mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly contraction in retail sales indicates collapsing consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when retail sales drop exceeds the 2% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment growth and rising unemployment rates signal labor market deterioration and hidden slack, which compresses corporate profit margins and produces negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when unemployment rises while payrolls grow, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above its 2017 baseline during a tightening cycle confirms resilient manufacturing output, which supports near-term equity momentum and yields positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations when industrial production exceeds 104 during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts reflect weakening residential investment and deteriorating household balance sheets, which systematically drag on aggregate demand and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward returns show a positive or zero correlation with housing starts declines over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price indices exceeding 295 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 295, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant quarterly GDP growth combined with falling retail sales confirms a demand shock that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields a positive predictive accuracy below 40% for 20-day forward returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates below 4.0% during an early expansion phase indicate tight labor markets that support wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations when unemployment stays below 4.0% during early expansion phases.
